description: Cross-Domain Ambiguity Benchmark — 24 seeds with ambiguous terms (network, tree, memory, model, kernel, port, pipeline, table, branch) across different CS domains. 15 queries testing disambiguation.

weakQueries.query: DDR5 RAM bandwidth and the memory hierarchy
weakQueries.analysis: Consistently misses a-pipe-cpu (CPU pipeline, grade 1). Memory terms dominate, CPU pipeline connection too weak for embedding model.

weakQueries.query: Using tables to store and look up data efficiently
weakQueries.analysis: Retrieves a-table-db and a-table-hash but misses a-table-route (grade 1). Routing tables too far from storage/lookup semantics.

keyFindings: Reranker provides strong disambiguation lift: vector_rerank Recall 0.922 vs vector 0.844 (+9.2%)
MRR is perfect for 3/4 modes — system consistently gets the top result right even under ambiguity
Hybrid_rerank degrades MRR to 0.933 — BM25 keyword matching can introduce noise on ambiguous terms
Ambiguous queries (a-q07 through a-q12) generally perform well — model handles polysemy better than expected
Category boost helps: category-tagged seeds in the same ambiguity group are correctly separated
